function_explanation: Adds complex Gaussian noise to data, where the noise magnitude is proportional to the amplitude of each element.
function_docstring:
Add complex Gaussian noise to data with magnitude relative to the signal amplitude.

Generates complex Gaussian noise where both real and imaginary components
are scaled by the local amplitude of the input data. This creates
signal-dependent noise that is stronger where the signal is stronger.

Args:
    data: Input array of complex or real values. Can be of any shape.
        The amplitude (absolute value) of each element determines the
        local noise magnitude.
    noise_level: A scalar factor controlling the noise intensity. The
        standard deviation of the noise at each point is proportional
        to noise_level * |data|.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: Complex array with the same shape as the input data,
        containing the original data plus complex Gaussian noise. The
        noise has real and imaginary parts that are independently drawn
        from Gaussian distributions with standard deviation equal to
        noise_level * amplitude at each point.
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Create some complex signal data
data = np.array([1+2j, 3+4j, 5+6j])

# Add 10% relative noise
noisy_data = add_noise(data, noise_level=0.1)

# For a 2D array
data_2d = np.random.randn(10, 20) + 1j * np.random.randn(10, 20)
noisy_data_2d = add_noise(data_2d, noise_level=0.05)
